backend_metrics.name: P132/P133 8-frame DROID-SLAM smoke
backend_metrics.scope: 8-frame bounded TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1 window, DROID-SLAM frontend/trajectory filler, global BA disabled because the smoke window is too short for proximity edges.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: This proves the raw-vs-masked backend and evo evaluation path is executable. It does not prove masked input improves full-trajectory SLAM, map quality, or navigation.
backend_metrics.interpretation: On this tiny smoke window, raw and masked trajectories are effectively tied. The result should be reported as pipeline closure, not as a performance improvement claim.

backend_metrics.name: P134 64-frame DROID-SLAM global BA
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame bounded TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1 window, DROID-SLAM with global BA enabled, Sim(3) alignment and scale correction in evo.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: This proves a larger bounded DROID-SLAM raw-vs-masked backend run with global BA is executable. It does not prove masked input improves full-trajectory SLAM, map quality, or navigation.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Raw and masked trajectories are effectively tied on this 64-frame bounded global-BA run. The single-frame dynamic mask has negligible effect at this window scale.

backend_metrics.name: P135 existing semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from existing forklift semantic frontend masks.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: Existing semantic masks are connected to the backend, but their temporal coverage is still sparse. The next step is dynamic-mask generation or tracking across more of the trajectory.

backend_metrics.name: P136 temporal mask stress test
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built by nearest-frame temporal propagation of existing semantic forklift masks within +/-8 frames plus 4 px dilation.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This is a diagnostic stress test, not a true detector output. It tests whether increasing temporal mask coverage makes the backend sensitive to dynamic masking.

backend_metrics.name: P137 optical-flow mask stress test
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built by dense optical-flow propagation of existing semantic forklift masks within +/-8 frames plus 4 px dilation.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This is a diagnostic stress test, not a true detector output. It tests whether optical-flow mask propagation improves over nearest-frame copying for backend sensitivity.

backend_metrics.name: P138 first-eight real semantic masks
backend_metrics.scope: 64-frame TorWIC Aisle_CW_Run_1, DROID-SLAM global BA, masked input built from existing per-frame frontend forklift masks on frames 000000-000007.
backend_metrics.claim_boundary: Metrics report bounded backend behavior only; they do not by themselves prove navigation benefit or full-map quality improvement.
backend_metrics.interpretation: This uses existing real frontend masks across the first eight frames rather than propagation. It tests whether denser true semantic masks change the backend trajectory metrics.
